title: Models of Federalism
lead: Three modes of Centre–State relations — ==cooperative==, ==competitive== and ==confrontational== — layered over a Constitution with a deliberately ==strong unitary spine==.

head: Aspect
Cooperative
Competitive
Confrontational
rows: Centre, states and states inter se ==compete for resources, investment, reforms==.
Adversarial relationship — often from ==central overstepping== into state domains.
Vertical — central dominance breeds disputes and resistance.
Implied: ==Inter-State Council, GST Council, NITI Aayog==.
Disputes over constitutional boundaries, misuse of concurrent powers.
GST Council · NITI Aayog · COVID-19 response · Ayushman Bharat
==EoDB rankings== · Vibrant Gujarat · state schemes (KALIA, Rythu Bandhu) · FDI races
==Art 370 revocation== · farm laws · Governor's role in TN · ==GST compensation disputes==
Can highlight state rights; judicial clarification of powers.

head: Feature
Basis
Example
rows: Defence, foreign relations, currency exclusively Union.
Parliament legislates on State subjects; state executive taken over.
Reservation of state bills for the President.
Collectors and SPs belong to AIS.
CSS funding + guidelines; ==cesses outside the divisible pool==.
ECI conducts state elections; ==CAG audits states==.
Union directions to states; GST Council recommends rates.
